title: Past ruin'd Ilion Helen lives,
content:
Past ruin'd Ilion Helen lives,
    Alcestis rises from the shades;
    Verse calls them forth; 'tis verse that gives
    Immortal youth to mortal maids.

    Soon shall Oblivion's deepening veil
    Hide all the peopled hills you see,
    The gay, the proud, while lovers hail
    These many summers you and me.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Walter Savage Landor, titled "Past ruin'd Ilion Helen lives,", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
